In-place JSONL row replacements (2 inserts / 2 deletions, same IDs):

           as another agent picked it up.
  - df5uj  Pin fcp-core ConnectorPlanState transitions — closure metadata
           refreshed (shipped at ff32cc6e7 as ConnectorLifecycleState
           transitions pin).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-58x","title":"fcp.jira: Sprint and board management","description":"# fcp.jira: Sprint and board management (FCP2)

## Goal
Implement Jira Agile operations for **boards** and **sprints** so agents can manage sprint workflows safely under FCP2 capability + approval enforcement.

This bead is specifically about Jira Software (Agile) surfaces (boards, sprints, backlog), not generic issue CRUD.

## Scope
### Read operations (low risk)
- List boards visible to the principal.
- Get board configuration (type, filters, location).
- List sprints for a board; get sprint metadata.
- List issues in a sprint/backlog (pagination; stable ordering).

### Write operations (medium/high risk)
- Create sprint (board-scoped).
- Start / complete sprint.
- Move issues into/out of sprint and backlog.

### Constraints
- Must work in both Jira Cloud and Server/DC (via `flywheel_connectors-3a9`).
- Must respect project permissions and Jira Software license availability.

## Capability model (illustrative)
Capability IDs MUST NOT encode hosts/ports.
- `jira.boards.read`
- `jira.sprints.read`
- `jira.sprints.write` (create/start/complete)
- `jira.sprints.assign_issues` (move issues)

Danger/approval guidance:
- Starting/completing sprints should be **approval-gated** by policy in many orgs.
  - Emit DecisionReceipt reason codes that are specific (e.g., `JIRA_SPRINT_START_REQUIRES_APPROVAL`).

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to the Jira base hostname.
- For write ops: emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
  - board_id / sprint_id / issue_keys (IDs only; no secret data)
  - request correlation id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.board.list`
  - `jira.board.get`
  - `jira.sprint.list`
  - `jira.sprint.get`
  - `jira.sprint.create`
  - `jira.sprint.start`
  - `jira.sprint.complete`
  - `jira.sprint.issues`
  - `jira.sprint.move_issues`
  - `jira.backlog.move_issues`
- Robust pagination helpers (bounded memory; streaming-friendly).
- Clear error mapping when Jira Software APIs are unavailable.

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Mock fixtures for Agile endpoints:
  - board listing and sprint listing
  - start/complete sprint happy paths
  - permission denied
  - missing Jira Software (feature not enabled)
  - rate-limit + retry/backoff

### Compliance
- Ensure capability-gating is enforced for each operation.
- Ensure write operations produce receipts/audit events.

## Acceptance criteria
- A mock-only test suite covers both Cloud and Server/DC variants.
- All write operations are capability + (policy-configurable) approval gated.
- No assumptions about “global boards”; all operations are explicitly scoped.

Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

GooglePeopleConfig currently accepts a configure-supplied base_url directly, while GooglePeopleClient uses it to construct authenticated People API requests. Add fail-closed validation and regression tests for host pinning, localhost test allowance, and userinfo/query/fragment rejection, matching the existing Google Docs/Sheets/Drive/Calendar connector pattern.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:06:17.706875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:10:53.627891Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:10:53.624327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"comments":[{"id":2963,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dcww0","author":"jemanuel","text":"b876bc7e implements Google People base_url fail-closed validation for host pinning, localhost test endpoints, and userinfo/query/fragment rejection. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-people-94685 cargo test -p fcp-google-people (29 passed); cargo check -p fcp-google-people passed; cargo fmt --check --package fcp-google-people passed; ubs --only=rust connectors/google-people/src/connector.rs reported 0 critical findings, with existing test unwrap/assert inventory warnings.","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:10:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","title":"fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution","description":"# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dfpyd","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-74: fcp-host McpToolListEntry + McpServerCapabilities + McpToolCallRequest + McpToolCapability wire format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:46:11.618703Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T01:47:47.755540Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T01:47:47.755038Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3227,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dfpyd","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped 21 conformance tests in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/host_mcp_tool_capabilities_conformance.rs. Pinned MCP tools/list + tools/call surface + server-capabilities matrix. McpServerCapabilities::default==with_tools() (just-tools is documented default); with_tools() sets tools.list_changed=Some(true) leaves others None; full() advertises every capability with subscribe+list_changed; has_tools/has_resources/has_prompts predicates; listChanged camelCase at every level. McpLoggingCapability empty struct→'{}' (presence-indicator). McpClientCapabilities all-None→'{}'. McpToolListEntry input_schema→inputSchema camelCase + required name+inputSchema + skip-when-None description+annotations + full roundtrip. McpToolListResponse nextCursor camelCase. McpToolCallRequest serde(default) arguments→Null-when-absent + arbitrary JSON preservation. 21/21 green in 0.00s. Commit b944501c.","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:47:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dghs4","title":"fix-X fwc connector-activate benchmark must measure real connector startup","description":"mock-code-finder found production placeholder logic in crates/fwc/src/bench_cmd.rs: fwc bench connector-activate accepts --connector but bench_connector_activate ignores the binary and times JSON parsing/session-id construction while describing connector cold start. Replace the fake activation path with a real subprocess cold-start measurement when a connector binary path is supplied, while keeping a truthful synthetic fallback only for the default fixture path. Add focused tests so this cannot regress.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:47:13.650128Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:55:44.641628Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:55:44.641172Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","fwc","mock-code"],"comments":[{"id":3016,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dghs4","author":"jemanuel","text":"ca43a3a2d35fab69937e46f504a8c611d4010b2f implements real fwc connector-activate probing: --connector now starts the supplied binary, sends JSON-RPC health, parses the response, and fails on missing/invalid binaries instead of timing a fake activation path. The no-binary default is labeled as a synthetic fixture. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod2-fixed cargo test -p fwc connector_activate -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod2-fixed cargo check -p fwc --all-targets; git diff --check -- crates/fwc/src/bench_cmd.rs; ubs --only=rust crates/fwc/src/bench_cmd.rs (0 critical).","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:55:40Z"}]}
